name: Cloud Foundry Deployment
description: Deploying apps to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry runtime
whyQuestion: Why Cloud Foundry instead of traditional server deployment?
explanation: CF abstracts infrastructure — no server management, automatic scaling, blue-green deployments. You push code, CF handles the rest.

name: XSUAA Authentication
description: SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service
whyQuestion: Why XSUAA instead of building your own auth system?
explanation: XSUAA integrates with SAP BTP Identity and enables multi-tenant OAuth2 flows without building your own token infrastructure.

name: BTP Object Store (S3)
description: SAP BTP Object Store Service for file storage
whyQuestion: Why Object Store instead of the local filesystem?
explanation: CF apps are stateless — local files are lost on restart. Object Store is persistent, scalable, and survives app restaging.

name: SAP AI Core Inference
description: Running AI model inference via SAP AI Core
whyQuestion: Why AI Core instead of calling OpenAI/Anthropic APIs directly?
explanation: AI Core runs inside BTP infrastructure — data sovereignty, no data leaving the boundary, SAP-managed billing and compliance.

name: Express Middleware
whyQuestion: Why the middleware pattern instead of handling everything in route handlers?
explanation: Middleware separates cross-cutting concerns (auth, logging, CORS) from business logic — DRY, testable, composable.

name: Express Routing
whyQuestion: Why Express Router instead of one big handler file?
explanation: Router separates API domains, enables prefix mounting (/api/users, /api/orders), and keeps code navigable as the app grows.

name: Vite Bundling
description: Fast frontend build tool with HMR
whyQuestion: Why Vite instead of Webpack?
explanation: Vite uses native ES Modules in dev — no full bundle needed, so HMR in <1s instead of 10s+ with Webpack. Faster feedback loop.

name: CORS Handling
whyQuestion: Why CORS middleware instead of a proxy?
explanation: CORS is browser security — without correct headers, the browser blocks cross-origin requests. A proxy bypasses it, but CORS is the correct solution for public APIs.

name: Zustand State Management
whyQuestion: Why Zustand instead of Redux?
explanation: Zustand has 1/10th the boilerplate — no action/reducer ceremony, direct state updates, TypeScript-first with full inference.

name: React Hooks Lifecycle
whyQuestion: Why are useEffect dependencies important?
explanation: Missing dependencies cause stale closures — bugs that are hard to trace. The exhaustive-deps ESLint rule catches this, but understanding why matters.

name: React Component Composition
whyQuestion: Why composition instead of inheritance in React?
explanation: React components are functions — composition via props/children is more flexible and testable than class inheritance. It's how React is designed to work.

name: React Suspense Boundaries
whyQuestion: Why Suspense instead of isLoading state?
explanation: Suspense separates loading UI from data fetching — less boilerplate, better UX through automatic waterfall prevention and declarative loading states.

name: MCP Server Tool Definition
description: Defining tools in Model Context Protocol servers
whyQuestion: Why MCP tools instead of direct API calls?
explanation: MCP standardizes the tool interface — any LLM can use the same tools without vendor lock-in. One tool definition serves Claude, GPT, Gemini.

name: MCP Transport Types
description: stdio vs SSE transport in MCP
whyQuestion: Why stdio instead of SSE for local MCP servers?
explanation: stdio is simpler — no HTTP server needed, direct process communication. SSE is for remote servers or when multiple clients connect simultaneously.

name: MCP Client Connection
description: Connecting to MCP servers from clients
whyQuestion: Why use an MCP Client instead of calling tools directly?
explanation: The MCP Client abstracts transport — switch from stdio to SSE without code changes. Clean separation of connection management from tool usage.

name: Prisma Migrations
whyQuestion: Why Prisma migrations instead of raw SQL?
explanation: Migrations are versioned and reversible — no manual schema tracking, automatic TypeScript types generated from the schema. Schema changes are code-reviewed.

name: JSONL Append Pattern
whyQuestion: Why JSONL instead of a JSON array for logs?
explanation: JSONL is append-only — no file lock needed, no full re-read for appends. A JSON array requires atomic write for every new entry.

name: JWT Validation
whyQuestion: Why server-side JWT validation instead of trusting the client?
explanation: Client-side JWT decoding (without signature verification) is not auth — anyone can forge tokens. verify() checks the cryptographic signature.

name: Docker Containerization
whyQuestion: Why Docker instead of deploying directly?
explanation: Containers isolate dependencies — no 'works on my machine' problems. Reproducible builds, easy rollback, consistent dev/staging/prod environments.

name: Environment Variables
whyQuestion: Why environment variables instead of config files?
explanation: Env vars separate code from configuration (12-Factor App principle) — no secrets leaked in git, easy environment switching between dev/staging/prod.
